task: Craft a tweet about askr's core problems (context loss across sessions) without revealing the solution, paired with strategic account follows for reach
discussion_summary: User is one week from public launch of askr and wants to build momentum on X without spoiling the product. Rejected the initial 'both problems fixed' angle as too weak and solution-focused. Landed on a sarcastic opener ('building with claude makes your life so much easier') paired with three concrete pain points: switching machines, re-explaining to co-founders, repeated session setup. This positions askr as solving a real pattern, not just two edge cases. User also needs a curated follow list of accounts in the AI/dev builder space to naturally engage with relevant content.

accomplishments.what: Finalized tweet copy for X launch (sarcasm-driven, three-problem structure)
Compiled curated follow list of 10 high-signal accounts (@alexalbert__, @simonw, @swyx, @karpathy, @amasad, @emollick, @garrytan, @levelsio, @marc_louvion, @kunal0dha)
Identified honest reach strategy: reply to existing threads rather than cold posts, build authority before product launch

next_actions.action: Post the finalized tweet with the image (#4) to X immediately
next_actions.why: Tweet is locked in and ready; momentum matters before public launch in one week

next_actions.action: Follow the 10 curated accounts and spend 15 min/day reacting to their posts with genuine takes (no askr mentions yet)
next_actions.why: Builds audience and credibility in the AI/dev builder space before product reveal; natural engagement beats cold outreach

next_actions.action: Identify 3-5 active threads from @swyx, @levelsio, @marc_louvion on Claude/context/AI tooling and add a substantive reply
next_actions.why: Positions you as knowledgeable in the space; these accounts have the audience you want and threads are already warm

next_actions.action: One week before public launch, prepare a 'here's what I built' thread that connects back to this pain-point tweet
next_actions.why: Creates narrative arc: problem → silence → solution reveal; maximizes impact of launch announcement

decisions.decision: Use sarcasm opener ('building with claude makes your life so much easier') instead of earnest problem statement
decisions.reason: Lands better, signals you understand the irony, avoids sounding weak or whiny

decisions.decision: Expand from two problems to three (machine switching, co-founder handoff, repeated session setup) to show pattern recognition
decisions.reason: Two problems make askr look narrow; three establish that this is a systemic issue, not edge cases

decisions.decision: Do not reveal the solution in this tweet; keep it one week out
decisions.reason: User is still building; premature reveal kills momentum and looks unfinished

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Tweet: 'both problems I posted about? fixed. built a daemon that watches your claude session...'
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: I don't want to post a solution, cause askr isn't built yet, I think I am still a week out from public launch

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Tweet opening: 'third problem I kept hitting while building with claude...'
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: I think starting it like that makes it gay as hell

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Initial reach strategy: reply to threads + build authority before launch
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: Also, how do I get reach bro? be honest — implies user wanted a faster/different approach but accepted the honest answer

failed_approaches.approach: Positioning askr as solving 'two problems' (quota checkpoints + context limits)
failed_approaches.reason: User correctly identified this makes the product look narrow and weak; three problems establish a pattern

failed_approaches.approach: Earnest problem statement opening ('third problem I kept hitting...')
failed_approaches.reason: Tone was off; sarcasm opener lands better and signals self-awareness

relational_files.why: Phase 4 (Public Launch) was removed from roadmap this session; tweet strategy aligns with pre-launch positioning
